mnemonics.title: OS FUNCTIONS MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: PEMS STORAGE - Process management, Execution control, Memory management, Security, Task management, I/O management, Auxiliary storage, Resource allocation, Control of input/output, Data management

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM CALL TYPES
mnemonics.content: PDSFD - Process control, Data management, File management, Device management, System information, Communication, Protection

acronyms.usage: The fundamental software that manages computer resources
The primary processor that executes instructions
Set of functions programs use to request OS services
Visual interface allowing user interaction with icons and windows
Text-based interface where users type commands
Firmware that initializes hardware during boot process
The part that directly interacts with hardware resources

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an onion with USER at the outer layer, then SHELL, then KERNEL at the core, and HARDWARE at the center. Each layer provides services to the layer above it.
Think of OS as a AIR TRAFFIC CONTROLLER at an airport, directing planes (processes) to runways (resources) efficiently and preventing collisions.
USER VIEW shows a friendly face (windows, icons); SYSTEM VIEW shows complex machinery underneath (CPU scheduling, memory management). Same OS, different perspectives.
Think of bootstrap like JUMPING: computer wakes up (power on), reads first instruction (jump to BIOS), loads OS (like loading a program into memory), and starts running - this is the bootstrap.

analogies.analogy: Just as government provides services (security, infrastructure, rules) to citizens while managing resources (budget, personnel, buildings), OS provides services to programs while managing hardware resources.
Like a manager coordinating chefs (CPU), storage (pantry), and tables (memory) to serve customers (users), OS coordinates hardware components to serve user needs.
Just as brain controls body functions without conscious thought, kernel controls hardware operations automatically. Users interact with shell like they interact with world through senses.
System calls are like filling out FORMS at a government office - you request a service by submitting required information, and the system processes it and provides the service.
